title: Fixing React Hook State Update Bug

statement.markdown: Implement a React functional component that uses the useState hook to update a counter state variable. When the component mounts, it should display a counter value of 0. After every 2 seconds, it should increment the counter by 1. However, the current implementation contains a bug that prevents the counter from updating correctly. Your task is to identify and fix the bug to get the counter value updated correctly.
statement.outputFormat: The updated counter value should be printed to the console
statement.notes: Use the useState hook to update the state variable, and use the useEffect hook to schedule the state update every 2 seconds.

constraints.description: No specific constraints on the counter value

examples.explanation: The counter value should be printed to the console every 2 seconds

hints.text: Check the order of state updates in the useEffect hook

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing React Hook State Update Bug
editorial.intuition: The bug is likely due to an incorrect order of state updates in the useEffect hook.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute force approach is to update the state variable directly in the useEffect hook.
editorial.optimalSolution: To fix the bug, we can use the useState hook with the correct order of state updates, and use the useEffect hook to schedule the state update every 2 seconds.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected code ensures that the state variable is updated correctly every 2 seconds.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 1. Mount the component, initialize the counter value to 0. 2. Schedule the state update every 2 seconds using the useEffect hook. 3. Update the counter value correctly every 2 seconds.
commonMistakes: Not using the useState hook correctly
